documentation:
# crashlink

![workflow](https://github.com/N3rdL0rd/crashlink/actions/workflows/python-package.yml/badge.svg)

Pure Python HashLink bytecode parser/disassembler/decompiler/modding tool

> [!WARNING]
> This project is under active development. Breaking changes may be made to APIs with zero notice.

## Features

- Pure Python with zero dependencies, integrates nicely in a lot of places (IDAPython compatible!)
- Allows values to be externally modified and reserialised through a scriptable interface
- A very nice little CLI with [hlbc](https://github.com/Gui-Yom/hlbc)-compatible mode.

## Installation

```bash
pip install crashlink
```

Optionally, install `tqdm` for progress bars when parsing large files:

```bash
pip install crashlink[tqdm]
```

You also need to have Graphviz installed to generate control flow graphs. On most *nix systems, on Windows (with Chocolatey or Scoop), and on MacOS (with Homebrew), you can install it with your package manager under `graphviz`.

- Windows: `choco install graphviz`
- MacOS: `brew install graphviz`
- Debian: `sudo apt install graphviz`
- Arch: `sudo pacman -S graphviz`
- Fedora: `sudo dnf install graphviz`

## Usage

Either:

```py
from crashlink import *
code = Bytecode.from_path("path/to/file.hl")
for func in code.functions:
    if func.findex.value == 22 or func.findex.value == 240: # typical entry points that the compiler generates
        print(disasm.func(code, func))
# > f@22 static $Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
# > Reg types:
# >   0. Void
# >
# > Ops:
# >   0. Ret             {'ret': 0}                                       return
```

Or:

```txt
$ crashlink path/to/file.hl # or python -m crashlink
crashlink> funcs
f@22 static Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
f@23 Clazz.method (Clazz) -> I32 (from Clazz.hx)
crashlink> fn 22
f@22 static Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
Reg types:
  0. Void

Ops:
  0. Ret             {'ret': 0}                                       return
```

## Development

> [!NOTE]
> This project is configured for the [just](https://just.systems/) command runner. If you don't have it installed, you can still run the commands in the `justfile` manually, but I don't recommend it.

For development purposes, you can clone the repo, install development dependencies, and run the tests:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/N3rdL0rd/crashlink
cd crashlink
# optionally, create and activate a venv here.
just install # or pip install -e .[dev]
just test # or pytest
```

Before committing, please run `just dev` to format the code, run tests, and generate documentation in `docs/`. If you're adding new features to the core serialisation/deserialisation code (`core.py`), please also add a test case in `tests/haxe/` for the new language feature you're adding. If you're adding a feature to the decompiler or disassembler, please add a normal test case (in Python) in `tests/` that tests the new feature.

Pull requests are always welcome! For major changes, please open an issue first to discuss what you would like to change.

You can use the following pre-defined commands with `just`:

- `just dev`: Run tests, format code, and generate documentation.
- `just build`: Build the package.
- `just install`: Install development dependencies and the package in editable mode.
- `just build-tests`: Build test samples.
- `just test`: Run tests.
- `just format`: Format code.
- `just docs`: Generate documentation.
- `just check`: Run static analysis/typechecking.
- `just clean`: Clean up build artifacts.
- `just profile`: Run the test suite with cProfile and then open the results in a browser.
- `just serve-docs`: Serve the documentation locally.

## Architecture

![Architecture](docs/static/flow.svg)

> [!NOTE]
> IR and the IR optimization layers have not yet been fully implemented.

## Credits

- Thank you to [Gui-Yom](https://github.com/Gui-Yom) for writing hlbc and for maintaining documentation on the HashLink bytecode format, as well as for providing tests and helping me during development.
- Thank you to [Haxe Foundation](https://haxe.org/) for creating the HashLink VM and the Haxe programming language.
- And a big thank you to you, dear user, for being at least partially interested in this project.

:heart: N3rdL0rd

modules.name: crashlink.opcodes
documentation: Definitions of all 98 supported opcodes in the HashLink VM.

variables.name: crashlink.opcodes.opcodes
documentation:
Definitions of all 98 supported opcodes in the HashLink VM.

Basic Operations:

- Mov: Copy value from src into dst register (`dst = src`) 
- Int: Load i32 constant from pool into dst (`dst = @ptr`)
- Float: Load f64 constant from pool into dst (`dst = @ptr`)
- Bool: Set boolean value in dst (`dst = true/false`)
- Bytes: Load byte array from constant pool into dst (`dst = @ptr`)
- String: Load string from constant pool into dst (`dst = @ptr`)
- Null: Set dst register to null (`dst = null`)

Arithmetic:

- Add: Add two numbers (`dst = a + b`)
- Sub: Subtract two numbers (`dst = a - b`) 
- Mul: Multiply two numbers (`dst = a * b`)
- SDiv: Signed division (`dst = a / b`)
- UDiv: Unsigned division (`dst = a / b`)
- SMod: Signed modulo (`dst = a % b`)
- UMod: Unsigned modulo (`dst = a % b`)

Bitwise:

- Shl: Left shift (`dst = a << b`)
- SShr: Signed right shift (`dst = a >> b`)
- UShr: Unsigned right shift (`dst = a >>> b`)
- And: Bitwise AND (`dst = a & b`)
- Or: Bitwise OR (`dst = a | b`)
- Xor: Bitwise XOR (`dst = a ^ b`)
- Neg: Negate value (`dst = -src`)
- Not: Boolean NOT (`dst = !src`)

Increment/Decrement:

- Incr: Increment value (`dst++`)
- Decr: Decrement value (`dst--`)

Function Calls:

- Call0: Call function with no args (`dst = fun()`)
- Call1: Call function with 1 arg (`dst = fun(arg0)`)
- Call2: Call function with 2 args (`dst = fun(arg0, arg1)`)
- Call3: Call function with 3 args (`dst = fun(arg0, arg1, arg2)`)
- Call4: Call function with 4 args (`dst = fun(arg0, arg1, arg2, arg3)`)
- CallN: Call function with N args (`dst = fun(args...)`)
- CallMethod: Call method with N args (`dst = obj.field(args...)`)
- CallThis: Call this method with N args (`dst = this.field(args...)`)
- CallClosure: Call closure with N args (`dst = fun(args...)`)

Closures:

- StaticClosure: Create closure from function (`dst = fun`)
- InstanceClosure: Create closure from object method (`dst = obj.fun`)
- VirtualClosure: Create closure from object field (`dst = obj.field`)

Global Variables:

- GetGlobal: Get global value (`dst = @global`)
- SetGlobal: Set global value (`@global = src`)

Fields:

- Field: Get object field (`dst = obj.field`)  
- SetField: Set object field (`obj.field = src`)
- GetThis: Get this field (`dst = this.field`)
- SetThis: Set this field (`this.field = src`)
- DynGet: Get dynamic field (`dst = obj[field]`)
- DynSet: Set dynamic field (`obj[field] = src`)

Control Flow:

- JTrue: Jump if true (`if cond jump by offset`)
- JFalse: Jump if false (`if !cond jump by offset`)
- JNull: Jump if null (`if reg == null jump by offset`)
- JNotNull: Jump if not null (`if reg != null jump by offset`)
- JSLt/JSGte/JSGt/JSLte: Signed comparison jumps
- JULt/JUGte: Unsigned comparison jumps 
- JNotLt/JNotGte: Negated comparison jumps
- JEq: Jump if equal (`if a == b jump by offset`)
- JNotEq: Jump if not equal (`if a != b jump by offset`)
- JAlways: Unconditional jump
- Label: Target for backward jumps (loops)
- Switch: Multi-way branch based on integer value

Type Conversions:

- ToDyn: Convert to dynamic type (`dst = (dyn)src`)
- ToSFloat: Convert to signed float (`dst = (float)src`)
- ToUFloat: Convert to unsigned float (`dst = (float)src`) 
- ToInt: Convert to int (`dst = (int)src`)
- SafeCast: Safe type cast with runtime check
- UnsafeCast: Unchecked type cast
- ToVirtual: Convert to virtual type

Exception Handling:

- Ret: Return from function (`return ret`)
- Throw: Throw exception 
- Rethrow: Rethrow exception
- Trap: Setup try-catch block
- EndTrap: End try-catch block
- NullCheck: Throw if null (`if reg == null throw`)

Memory Operations:

- GetI8: Read i8 from bytes (`dst = bytes[index]`)
- GetI16: Read i16 from bytes (`dst = bytes[index]`)
- GetMem: Read from memory (`dst = bytes[index]`)
- GetArray: Get array element (`dst = array[index]`)
- SetI8: Write i8 to bytes (`bytes[index] = src`)
- SetI16: Write i16 to bytes (`bytes[index] = src`) 
- SetMem: Write to memory (`bytes[index] = src`)
- SetArray: Set array element (`array[index] = src`)

Objects:

- New: Allocate new object (`dst = new typeof(dst)`)
- ArraySize: Get array length (`dst = len(array)`)
- Type: Get type object (`dst = type ty`)
- GetType: Get value's type (`dst = typeof src`)
- GetTID: Get type ID (`dst = typeof src`)

References:

- Ref: Create reference (`dst = &src`)
- Unref: Read reference (`dst = *src`)
- Setref: Write reference (`*dst = src`)
- RefData: Get reference data
- RefOffset: Get reference with offset

Enums:

- MakeEnum: Create enum variant (`dst = construct(args...)`)
- EnumAlloc: Create enum with defaults (`dst = construct()`)
- EnumIndex: Get enum tag (`dst = variant of value`)
- EnumField: Get enum field (`dst = (value as construct).field`)
- SetEnumField: Set enum field (`value.field = src`)

Other:

- Assert: Debug break
- Nop: No operation
- Prefetch: CPU memory prefetch hint
- Asm: Inline x86 assembly

If you want to see a more detailed pseudocode for any given instance of `Opcode`, you can use `crashlink.disasm.pseudo_from_op()` to get a human-readable representation of the operation.

If you're using the CLI in patch mode, you'll see opcodes in a format like:

```hlasm
Int. 0. 0
Int. 2. 1
GetGlobal. 3. 3
Add. 4. 0. 2
Sub. 5. 0. 2
Mul. 6. 0. 2
ToSFloat. 8. 0
ToSFloat. 9. 2
SDiv. 8. 8. 9
SMod. 7. 0. 2
Shl. 10. 0. 2
JSLt. 0. 2. 2
Bool. 11. False
JAlways. 1
Bool. 11. True
JSLt. 0. 2. 2
Bool. 12. False
JAlways. 1
Bool. 12. True
Nop.
CallN. [1, 2, 3]
Ret. 1
```

When writing opcodes in this format, seperate each argument to the opcode with a period. For opcodes that require lists, pass them in JSON format, e.g. `CallN. [reg, reg, reg, reg]`.

modules.name: crashlink.errors
documentation: crashlink-specific errors.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.CrashlinkError
documentation: Base exception class for all errors raised by crashlink.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.MalformedBytecode
documentation: Raised when malformed bytecode is deserialised.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.NoMagic
documentation: Raised when no magic b"HLB" can be found in a file.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.InvalidOpCode
documentation: Raised when an invalid opcode is encountered.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.FailedSerialisation
documentation: Raised when reserialisation of bytecode fails.

classes.name: crashlink.errors.DecompError
documentation: Raised when an error occurs during decompilation.

modules.name: crashlink
documentation:
Pure Python HashLink bytecode parser/disassembler/decompiler/modding tool

## Features

- Pure Python with zero dependencies, integrates nicely in a lot of places (IDAPython compatible!)
- Allows values to be externally modified and reserialised through a scriptable interface
- A very nice little CLI with [hlbc](https://github.com/Gui-Yom/hlbc)-compatible mode.

## Installation

```bash
pip install crashlink
```

Optionally, install `tqdm` for progress bars when parsing large files:

```bash
pip install crashlink[tqdm]
```

You also need to have Graphviz installed to generate control flow graphs. On most *nix systems, on Windows (with Chocolatey or Scoop), and on MacOS (with Homebrew), you can install it with your package manager under `graphviz`.

- Windows: `choco install graphviz`
- MacOS: `brew install graphviz`
- Debian: `sudo apt install graphviz`
- Arch: `sudo pacman -S graphviz`
- Fedora: `sudo dnf install graphviz`

## Usage

Either:

```py
from crashlink import *
code = Bytecode.from_path("path/to/file.hl")
for func in code.functions:
    if func.findex.value == 22 or func.findex.value == 240: # typical entry points that the compiler generates
        print(disasm.func(code, func))
# > f@22 static $Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
# > Reg types:
# >   0. Void
# >
# > Ops:
# >   0. Ret             {'ret': 0}                                       return
```

Or:

```bash
$ crashlink path/to/file.hl # or python -m crashlink
crashlink> funcs
f@22 static Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
f@23 Clazz.method (Clazz) -> I32 (from Clazz.hx)
crashlink> fn 22
f@22 static Clazz.main () -> Void (from Clazz.hx)
Reg types:
  0. Void

Ops:
  0. Ret             {'ret': 0}                                       return
```

## Development

> Note:
> This project is configured for the [just](https://just.systems/) command runner. If you don't have it installed, you can still run the commands in the `justfile` manually, but I don't recommend it.

For development purposes, you can clone the repo, install development dependencies, and run the tests:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/N3rdL0rd/crashlink
cd crashlink
# optionally, create and activate a venv here.
just install # or pip install -e .[dev]
just test # or pytest
```

Before committing, please run `just dev` to format the code, run tests, and generate documentation in `docs/`. If you're adding new features to the core serialisation/deserialisation code (`crashlink.core`), please also add a test case in `tests/haxe/` for the new language feature you're adding. If you're adding a feature to the decompiler or disassembler, please add a normal test case (in Python) in `tests/` that tests the new feature.

Pull requests are always welcome! For major changes, please open an issue first to discuss what you would like to change.

You can use the following pre-defined commands with `just`:

- `just dev`: Run tests, format code, and generate documentation.
- `just build`: Build the package.
- `just install`: Install development dependencies and the package in editable mode.
- `just build-tests`: Build test samples.
- `just test`: Run tests.
- `just format`: Format code.
- `just docs`: Generate documentation.
- `just check`: Run static analysis/typechecking.
- `just clean`: Clean up build artifacts.
- `just profile`: Run the test suite with cProfile and then open the results in a browser.
- `just serve-docs`: Serve the documentation locally.

## Credits

- Thank you to [Gui-Yom](https://github.com/Gui-Yom) for writing hlbc and for maintaining documentation on the HashLink bytecode format, as well as for providing tests and helping me during development.
- Thank you to [Haxe Foundation](https://haxe.org/) for creating the HashLink VM and the Haxe programming language.
- And a big thank you to you, dear user, for being at least partially interested in this project.

❤ N3rdL0rd

modules.name: crashlink.globals
documentation: Global configuration.

functions.name: crashlink.globals.dbg_print
documentation: Print a message if DEBUG is True.

functions.name: crashlink.globals.tell
documentation: Hex-formatted tell of a file.

functions.name: crashlink.globals.fmt_bytes
documentation: Format bytes into a human-readable string.

variables.name: crashlink.globals.VERSION
documentation: The version of crashlink.

variables.name: crashlink.globals.LONG_VERSION
documentation: String displayed in the help message for the CLI.

variables.name: crashlink.globals.DEBUG
documentation: Whether to enable certain features meant only for development or debugging of crashlink.

modules.name: crashlink.__main__
documentation: Entrypoint for the crashlink CLI.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.cmd_help
documentation: Help command, lists available commands from `COMMANDS`.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.cmd_funcs
documentation: Prints all functions and natives in the bytecode. If `std` is passed as an argument, it will include stdlib functions.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.cmd_entry
documentation: Prints the entrypoint of the bytecode.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.cmd_fn
documentation: Disassembles a function to pseudocode by findex.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.cmd_cfg
documentation: Renders a control flow graph for a given findex and attempts to open it in the default image viewer.s

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.handle_cmd
documentation: Handles a command.

functions.name: crashlink.__main__.main
documentation: Main entrypoint.

variables.name: crashlink.__main__.COMMANDS
documentation: List of CLI commands.

modules.name: crashlink.disasm
documentation: Human-readable disassembly of opcodes and utilities to work at a relatively low level with HashLink bytecode.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.get_proto_for
documentation: Gets the proto for a standalone function index.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.get_field_for
documentation: Gets the field for a standalone function index.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.type_name
documentation: Generates a human-readable name for a type.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.full_func_name
documentation: Generates a human-readable name for a function or native.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.type_to_haxe
documentation: Maps internal HashLink type names to Haxe type names.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.func_header
documentation: Generates a human-readable header for a function.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.native_header
documentation: Generates a human-readable header for a native function.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.is_std
documentation: Checks if a function is from the standard library. This is a heuristic and is a bit broken still.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.is_static
documentation: Checks if a function is static.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.pseudo_from_op
documentation: Generates pseudocode disassembly from an opcode.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.fmt_op
documentation: Formats an opcode into a table row.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.func
documentation: Generates a human-readable printout and disassembly of a function or native.

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.to_asm
documentation:
Dumps a list of opcodes to a human-readable(-ish) assembly format.

Eg.:
```txt
Int. 0. 0
Int. 2. 1
GetGlobal. 3. 3
Add. 4. 0. 2
Sub. 5. 0. 2
Mul. 6. 0. 2
ToSFloat. 8. 0
ToSFloat. 9. 2
SDiv. 8. 8. 9
SMod. 7. 0. 2
Shl. 10. 0. 2
JSLt. 0. 2. 2
Bool. 11. False
JAlways. 1
Bool. 11. True
JSLt. 0. 2. 2
Bool. 12. False
JAlways. 1
Bool. 12. True
Ret. 1
```

functions.name: crashlink.disasm.from_asm
documentation: Reads and parses a list of opcodes from a human-readable(-ish) assembly format. See `to_asm`.

modules.name: crashlink.decomp
documentation: Decompilation, IR and control flow graph generation

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.CFNode
documentation: A control flow node.

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.CFOptimizer
documentation: Base class for control flow graph optimizers.

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.CFJumpThreader
documentation: Thread jumps to reduce the number of nodes in the graph.

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.CFDeadCodeEliminator
documentation: Remove unreachable code blocks

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.CFGraph

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.CFGraph.build
documentation: Build the control flow graph.

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.CFGraph.graph
documentation: Generate DOT format graph visualization.
A control flow graph.

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRBlock
documentation: A basic unit block of IR. Contains a list of IRStatements, and can contain other IRBlocks.

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRExpression

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.IRExpression.get_type
documentation: Get the type of value this expression produces
Abstract base class for expressions that produce a value

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRAssign
documentation: Assignment of an expression result to a local variable

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRCall
documentation: Function call expression

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRBoolExpr
documentation: Base class for boolean expressions

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRConst
documentation: Represents a constant value expression

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRConditional
documentation: A conditional statement

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRReturn
documentation: Return statement

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRTrace
documentation: Trace statement

classes.name: crashlink.decomp.IRFunction

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.IRFunction._lift
documentation: Lift function to IR

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.IRFunction._optimize
documentation: Optimize the IR

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.IRFunction._name_locals
documentation: Name locals based on debug info

methods.name: crashlink.decomp.IRFunction._lift_block
documentation: Lift a control flow node to an IR block
Intermediate representation of a function.

modules.name: crashlink.core
documentation:
Core bytecode format definitions.

This module contains the definitions for HashLink bytecode structures, as well as the serialisation
and deserialisation methods for them. You probably don't need to use too much of this file directly,
besides Bytecode, Opcode, and Function. The decompiler will take care of a lot of abstraction for
you.

variables.name: crashlink.core.T
documentation: HACK: easier than reimplementing deserialise for each subclass

classes.name: crashlink.core.Serialisable
documentation: Base class for all serialisable objects.
TODO: rewrite all ABCs like this to use the PEP 3119 `abc` module

classes.name: crashlink.core.RawData
documentation: A block of raw data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.SerialisableInt
documentation: Integer of the specified byte length.

classes.name: crashlink.core.SerialisableF64
documentation: A standard 64-bit float.

classes.name: crashlink.core.VarInt

class_variables.name: crashlink.core.VarInt._struct_short
documentation: big-endian unsigned short

class_variables.name: crashlink.core.VarInt._struct_medium
documentation: big-endian unsigned int (for 3 bytes)
Variable-length integer - can be 1, 2, or 4 bytes.

classes.name: crashlink.core.ResolvableVarInt
documentation: Base class for resolvable VarInts. Call `resolve` to get a direct reference to the object it points to.

classes.name: crashlink.core.fIndex
documentation: Abstract class based on VarInt to represent a distinct function index instead of just an arbitrary number.

classes.name: crashlink.core.tIndex
documentation: Reference to a type in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.gIndex
documentation: Reference to a global object in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.strRef
documentation: Reference to a string in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.intRef
documentation: Reference to an integer in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.floatRef
documentation: Reference to a float in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.bytesRef
documentation: Reference to a byte string in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.fieldRef
documentation: Reference to a field in an object definition.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Reg
documentation: Reference to a register in the bytecode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.InlineBool
documentation: Inline boolean value.

classes.name: crashlink.core.VarInts
documentation: List of VarInts.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Regs
documentation: List of references to registers.

classes.name: crashlink.core.StringsBlock
documentation: Block of strings in the bytecode. Contains a list of strings and their lengths.

classes.name: crashlink.core.BytesBlock
documentation: Block of bytes in the bytecode. Contains a list of byte strings and their lengths.

classes.name: crashlink.core.TypeDef
documentation: Abstract class for all type definition fields.

classes.name: crashlink.core._NoDataType
documentation: Base typedef for types with no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Void
documentation: Void type, no data. Used to discard data (eg. reg: Void = call f@**).

classes.name: crashlink.core.U8
documentation: Unsigned 8-bit integer type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.U16
documentation: Unsigned 16-bit integer type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.I32
documentation: Signed 32-bit integer type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.I64
documentation: Signed 64-bit integer type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.F32
documentation: 32-bit float type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.F64
documentation: 64-bit float type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Bool
documentation: Boolean type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Bytes
documentation: Bytes type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Dyn
documentation: Dynamic type, no data. Can store any type of data in a typed register as a pointer.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Fun
documentation: Stores metadata about a function (signatures). When referenced in conjunction with a Proto or Method, it can be used to reconstruct the full function signature. See `crashlink.disasm.func_header` for a working reference.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Field
documentation: Represents a field in a class definition.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Proto
documentation: Represents a prototype of a function

classes.name: crashlink.core.Binding
documentation: Represents a binding of a field to a class.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Obj
documentation:
Represents a class definition. Contains:

- name: strRef
- super: tIndex
- _global: gIndex
- nfields: VarInt
- nprotos: VarInt
- nbindings: VarInt
- fields: List[Field]
- protos: List[Proto]
- bindings: List[Binding]

classes.name: crashlink.core.Array
documentation: Array type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.TypeType
documentation: Type wrapping a type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Ref
documentation: Memory reference to an instance of a type.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Virtual
documentation: Virtual type, used for virtual/abstract classes.

classes.name: crashlink.core.DynObj
documentation: Dynamic object type, no data.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Abstract
documentation: Abstract class type.

classes.name: crashlink.core.EnumConstruct
documentation: Construct of an enum.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Enum
documentation: Enum type.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Null
documentation: Null of a certain type.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Method
documentation: Method type, identical to Fun.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Struct
documentation: Struct type, identical to Obj.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Packed
documentation: Holds an inner type index.

Type definition:

- kind: SerialisableInt

classes.name: crashlink.core.Native
documentation:
Represents a native function.

- lib: strRef
- name: strRef
- type: tIndex
- findex: fIndex

classes.name: crashlink.core.Opcode
documentation: Represents an opcode.

classes.name: crashlink.core.fileRef
documentation: Reference to a file in the debug info.

classes.name: crashlink.core.DebugInfo

methods.name: crashlink.core.DebugInfo.flush_repeat
documentation: Helper function to handle repeat encoding.
Represents debug information for a function, encoded with a delta encoding scheme for compression.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Function
documentation: Represents a function in the bytecode. Due to the interesting ways in which HashLink works, this does not have a name or a signature, but rather a return type and a list of registers and opcodes.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Constant
documentation: Represents a bytecode constant.

classes.name: crashlink.core.Bytecode
documentation:
The main bytecode class. To read a bytecode file, use the `from_path` class method.

For more information about the overall structure, see [here](https://n3rdl0rd.github.io/ModDocCE/files/hlboot)
